title: I son't see my containers (podman ps --all)
body:
I am using RHEL 8.4

I installed Podman 3.2.3 with dnf install podman

I can ** run podman hello-world ** but other containers are not showing with ** podman ps --all **

** podman build -t mycontainer. ** I do not see any errors for the construction

** podman run mycontainer --name test -p 5900: 5900 ** I see the key
48f965gb7d08b3289813d1f517621203ea5d59e534b18b3981c5e50b04b73c70

as if the container was working

** podman ps --all **: have no containers

I have the same problem with fullroot and I don't see my containers on htop or systemctl


The podman project interests me a lot, but I can't get my containers to work. What is difficult is that I do not see any errors during execution. I don't know if I did something wrong. Is there a place I need to set it up?


Thank you!

comments.body: Please provide more details - a full `podman info` from the user you are running Podman as would help greatly. The complete output of `podman run --log-level=debug -t -i --rm fedora ls` and `podman ps --log-level=debug --all` would also be appreciated.

Thanks mheon for your quick answer.

By executing the commands you asked me, I see that the Fedora container has worked. 
I use containers with a Dockerfile. can the problem come from there?

I did the same test with my container (all the way down)

Thank you very much, I am learning a lot at the momentn :)

It seems like you are seeing the containers?


> ** podman run mycontainer --name test -p 5900: 5900 **

are you sure this command is correct?  Should it be `podman run --name test -p 5900: 5900 mycontainer`?

@FelixLahaie It looks like you run your container with `--rm`, that means it will be removed once it exits. If you remove `--rm` it should show the container with `podman ps -a`. I suspect there is a problem with your container application which is causing it to exit immediately.

Hello, giuseppe @giuseppe 

sorry, you right,   my command  `podman run --name test -p 5900: 5900 mycontainer` . I made a mistake when writing my issue.

Strangely, I see my container for only 1 second when I hurry to do the `podman ps`
